title: Analytics - Firmware Update Failed
scenario: Firmware Update Failed Firebase page does not display updated event counts or expected default elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description) after a DFU failure on a Poly Bluetooth device
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update.
4. Initiate a DFU (firmware update) on the connected device.
5. Cause the firmware update to fail (e.g., disconnect the device, interrupt Bluetooth, or force close the app during the update).
6. Wait a few hours for Firebase event counts to propagate.
7. Open the Firebase console and navigate to the 'Firmware Update Failed' event page.

8. Verify the page loads properly and contains the following default elements: Graph, Events in last 30 minutes, Event count by Country, Event count by Gender, Events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment.
9. Verify the page also contains the following additional elements: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description.
10. Check that the event count for the 'Firmware Update Failed' event and related elements has increased compared to before the failed DFU.
expected: The Firmware Update Failed Firebase page loads successfully and displays all default elements (Graph, Events in last 30 minutes, Event count by Country, Event count by Gender, Events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment) plus the additional elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description). After a failed DFU and a few hours of propagation time, the event count for the 'Firmware Update Failed' event and related elements increases.
